verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, vertente anno, eo tempore quo solent reges ad bella procedere, misit David Joab, et servos suos cum eo, et universum Israël, et vastaverunt filios Ammon, et obsederunt Rabba : David autem remansit in Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִי֩ לִתְשׁוּבַ֨ת הַשָּׁנָ֜ה לְעֵ֣ת ׀ צֵ֣את הַמְּלָאכִ֗ים וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח דָּוִ֡ד אֶת־יוֹאָב֩ וְאֶת־עֲבָדָ֨יו עִמּ֜וֹ וְאֶת־כׇּל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל וַיַּשְׁחִ֙תוּ֙ אֶת־בְּנֵ֣י עַמּ֔וֹן וַיָּצֻ֖רוּ עַל־רַבָּ֑ה וְדָוִ֖ד יוֹשֵׁ֥ב בִּירוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass at the return of the year, at the time when kings go forth to war, that David sent Joab and his servants with him, and all Israel, and they spoiled the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabba: but David remained in Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin se aika joutui vuodesta, jolla kuningasten tapa oli sotiin mennä, lähetti David Joabin ja palveliansa hänen kanssansa ja kaiken Israelin, hukuttamaan Ammonin lapsia, ja he piirittivät Rabban; mutta David jäi Jerusalemiin.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, after the year was expired, at the time when kings go forth to battle, that David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried still at Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum hæc agerentur, accidit ut surgeret David de strato suo post meridiem, et deambularet in solario domus regiæ : viditque mulierem se lavantem ex adverso super solarium suum : erat autem mulier pulchra valde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י ׀ לְעֵ֣ת הָעֶ֗רֶב וַיָּ֨קׇם דָּוִ֜ד מֵעַ֤ל מִשְׁכָּבוֹ֙ וַיִּתְהַלֵּךְ֙ עַל־גַּ֣ג בֵּית־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיַּ֥רְא אִשָּׁ֛ה רֹחֶ֖צֶת מֵעַ֣ל הַגָּ֑ג וְהָ֣אִשָּׁ֔ה טוֹבַ֥ת מַרְאֶ֖ה מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the mean time it happened that David arose from his bed after noon, and walked upon the roof of the king's house: And he saw from the roof of his house a woman washing herself, over against him: and the woman was very beautiful.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui, että David ehtoopuolella nousi vuoteeltansa, ja käyskenteli kuninkaan huoneen katon päällä, ja näki katon päältä vaimon pesevän itsiänsä; ja vaimo oli sangen ihana näkyänsä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in an eveningtide, that David arose from off his bed, and walked upon the roof of the king’s house: and from the roof he saw a woman washing herself; and the woman was very beautiful to look upon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit ergo rex, et requisivit quæ esset mulier. Nuntiatumque est ei quod ipsa esset Bethsabee filia Eliam, uxor Uriæ Hethæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח דָּוִ֔ד וַיִּדְרֹ֖שׁ לָאִשָּׁ֑ה וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הֲלוֹא־זֹאת֙ בַּת־שֶׁ֣בַע בַּת־אֱלִיעָ֔ם אֵ֖שֶׁת אוּרִיָּ֥ה הַֽחִתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king sent, and inquired who the woman was. And it was told him, that she was Bethsabee the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Urias the Hethite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David lähetti ja antoi kysyä vaimoa, ja sanoi: eikö tämä ole BatSeba, Eliamin tytär, Hetiläisen Urian emäntä.
verses.king_james: And David sent and enquired after the woman. And one said, Is not this Bath–sheba, the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?

verses.latin_vulgate: Missis itaque David nuntiis, tulit eam : quæ cum ingressa esset ad illum, dormivit cum ea : statimque sanctificata est ab immunditia sua,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַח֩ דָּוִ֨ד מַלְאָכִ֜ים וַיִּקָּחֶ֗הָ וַתָּב֤וֹא אֵלָיו֙ וַיִּשְׁכַּ֣ב עִמָּ֔הּ וְהִ֥יא מִתְקַדֶּ֖שֶׁת מִטֻּמְאָתָ֑הּ וַתָּ֖שׇׁב אֶל־בֵּיתָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David sent messengers, and took her, and she came in to him, and he slept with her: and presently she was purified from her uncleanness:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David lähetti sanansaattajat ja antoi tuoda hänen. Ja kuin hän tuli hänen tykönsä, makasi hän hänen kanssansa; mutta hän puhdisti itsensä saastaisuudestansa, ja palasi huoneesensa.
verses.king_james: And David sent messengers, and took her; and she came in unto him, and he lay with her; for she was purified from her uncleanness: and she returned unto her house.

verses.latin_vulgate: et reversa est domum suam concepto fœtu. Mittensque nuntiavit David, et ait : Concepi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֖הַר הָאִשָּׁ֑ה וַתִּשְׁלַח֙ וַתַּגֵּ֣ד לְדָוִ֔ד וַתֹּ֖אמֶר הָרָ֥ה אָנֹֽכִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she returned to her house having conceived. And she sent and told David, and said: I have conceived.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaimo tuli raskaaksi, ja lähetti ja ilmoitti sen Davidille, ja sanoi: minä olen raskaaksi tullut.
verses.king_james: And the woman conceived, and sent and told David, and said, I am with child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit autem David ad Joab, dicens : Mitte ad me Uriam Hethæum. Misitque Joab Uriam ad David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח דָּוִד֙ אֶל־יוֹאָ֔ב שְׁלַ֣ח אֵלַ֔י אֶת־אוּרִיָּ֖ה הַחִתִּ֑י וַיִּשְׁלַ֥ח יוֹאָ֛ב אֶת־אוּרִיָּ֖ה אֶל־דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David sent to Joab, saying: Send me Urias the Hethite. And Joab sent Urias to David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lähetti David Joabin tykö, sanoen: lähetä minun tyköni Uria Hetiläinen. Ja Joab lähetti Urian Davidin tykö.
verses.king_james: And David sent to Joab, saying, Send me Uriah the Hittite. And Joab sent Uriah to David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit Urias ad David. Quæsivitque David quam recte ageret Joab et populus, et quomodo administraretur bellum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֥א אוּרִיָּ֖ה אֵלָ֑יו וַיִּשְׁאַ֣ל דָּוִ֗ד לִשְׁל֤וֹם יוֹאָב֙ וְלִשְׁל֣וֹם הָעָ֔ם וְלִשְׁל֖וֹם הַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Urias came to David. And David asked how Joab did, and the people, and how the war was carried on.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Uria tuli hänen tykönsä, kysyi David: jos Joab ja kansa rauhassa olisivat ja jos sota menestyis?
verses.king_james: And when Uriah was come unto him, David demanded of him how Joab did, and how the people did, and how the war prospered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit David ad Uriam : Vade in domum tuam, et lava pedes tuos. Et egressus est Urias de domo regis, secutusque est eum cibus regius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר דָּוִד֙ לְא֣וּרִיָּ֔ה רֵ֥ד לְבֵיתְךָ֖ וּרְחַ֣ץ רַגְלֶ֑יךָ וַיֵּצֵ֤א אֽוּרִיָּה֙ מִבֵּ֣ית הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַתֵּצֵ֥א אַחֲרָ֖יו מַשְׂאַ֥ת הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Urias: Go into thy house, and wash thy feet. And Urias went out from the king's house, and there went out after him a mess of meat from the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi Urialle: mene huoneeses ja pese jalkas. Ja kuin Uria läksi kuninkaan huoneesta, seurasi häntä kohta kuninkaan herkku.
verses.king_james: And David said to Uriah, Go down to thy house, and wash thy feet. And Uriah departed out of the king’s house, and there followed him a mess of meat from the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivit autem Urias ante portam domus regiæ cum aliis servis domini sui, et non descendit ad domum suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֣ב אוּרִיָּ֗ה פֶּ֚תַח בֵּ֣ית הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ אֵ֖ת כׇּל־עַבְדֵ֣י אֲדֹנָ֑יו וְלֹ֥א יָרַ֖ד אֶל־בֵּיתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Urias slept before the gate of the king's house, with the other servants of his lord, and went not down to his own house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Uria pani levätä kuninkaan huoneen oven eteen, kussa kaikki hänen herransa palveliat makasivat, ja ei mennyt huoneesensa.
verses.king_james: But Uriah slept at the door of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and went not down to his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nuntiatumque est David a dicentibus : Non ivit Urias in domum suam. Et ait David ad Uriam : Numquid non de via venisti ? quare non descendisti in domum tuam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּגִּ֤דוּ לְדָוִד֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר לֹא־יָרַ֥ד אוּרִיָּ֖ה אֶל־בֵּית֑וֹ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד אֶל־אוּרִיָּ֗ה הֲל֤וֹא מִדֶּ֙רֶךְ֙ אַתָּ֣ה בָ֔א מַדּ֖וּעַ לֹא־יָרַ֥דְתָּ אֶל־בֵּיתֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it was told David by some that said: Urias went not to his house. And David said to Urias: Didst thou not come from thy journey? why didst thou not go down to thy house?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Davidille sanottiin: ei Uria ole mennyt huoneesensa, sanoi David Urialle: etkös tullut matkasta? miksi et mennyt huoneeses?
verses.king_james: And when they had told David, saying, Uriah went not down unto his house, David said unto Uriah, Camest thou not from thy journey? why then didst thou not go down unto thine house?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Urias ad David : Arca Dei et Israël et Juda habitant in papilionibus, et dominus meus Joab et servi domini mei super faciem terræ manent : et ego ingrediar domum meam, ut comedam et bibam, et dormiam cum uxore mea ? Per salutem tuam, et per salutem animæ tuæ, non faciam rem hanc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אוּרִיָּ֜ה אֶל־דָּוִ֗ד הָ֠אָר֠וֹן וְיִשְׂרָאֵ֨ל וִיהוּדָ֜ה יֹשְׁבִ֣ים בַּסֻּכּ֗וֹת וַאדֹנִ֨י יוֹאָ֜ב וְעַבְדֵ֤י אֲדֹנִי֙ עַל־פְּנֵ֤י הַשָּׂדֶה֙ חֹנִ֔ים וַאֲנִ֞י אָב֧וֹא אֶל־בֵּיתִ֛י לֶאֱכֹ֥ל וְלִשְׁתּ֖וֹת וְלִשְׁכַּ֣ב עִם־אִשְׁתִּ֑י חַיֶּ֙ךָ֙ וְחֵ֣י נַפְשֶׁ֔ךָ אִֽם־אֶעֱשֶׂ֖ה אֶת־הַדָּבָ֥ר הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Urias said to David: The ark of God and Israel and Juda dwell in tents, and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord abide upon the face of the earth: and shall I go into my house, to eat and to drink, and to sleep with my wife? By thy welfare and by the welfare of thy soul I will not do this thing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Uria sanoi Davidille: arkki, ja Israel ja Juuda makaavat majoissa, herrani Joab ja herrani palveliat makaavat kedolla, ja minä menisin huoneesen syömään, juomaan ja makaamaan emäntäni tykönä? Niin totta kuin sinä elät ja sinun sielus elää, en tee minä sitä.
verses.king_james: And Uriah said unto David, The ark, and Israel, and Judah, abide in tents; and my lord Joab, and the servants of my lord, are encamped in the open fields; shall I then go into mine house, to eat and to drink, and to lie with my wife? as thou livest, and as thy soul liveth, I will not do this thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait ergo David ad Uriam : Mane hic etiam hodie, et cras dimittam te. Mansit Urias in Jerusalem in die illa et altera :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד אֶל־אוּרִיָּ֗ה שֵׁ֥ב בָּזֶ֛ה גַּם־הַיּ֖וֹם וּמָחָ֣ר אֲשַׁלְּחֶ֑ךָּ וַיֵּ֨שֶׁב אוּרִיָּ֧ה בִירוּשָׁלַ֛͏ִם בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא וּמִֽמׇּחֳרָֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then David said to Urias: Tarry here to day, and to morrow I will send thee away. Urias tarried in Jerusalem that day and the next.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi Urialle: niin ole vielä tämä päivä tässä, huomenna lasken minä sinun menemään. Niin Uria oli sen päivän Jerusalemissa ja toisen päivän.
verses.king_james: And David said to Uriah, Tarry here to day also, and to morrow I will let thee depart. So Uriah abode in Jerusalem that day, and the morrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: et vocavit eum David ut comederet coram se et biberet, et inebriavit eum : qui egressus vespere, dormivit in strato suo cum servis domini sui, et in domum suam non descendit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָא־ל֣וֹ דָוִ֗ד וַיֹּ֧אכַל לְפָנָ֛יו וַיֵּ֖שְׁתְּ וַֽיְשַׁכְּרֵ֑הוּ וַיֵּצֵ֣א בָעֶ֗רֶב לִשְׁכַּ֤ב בְּמִשְׁכָּבוֹ֙ עִם־עַבְדֵ֣י אֲדֹנָ֔יו וְאֶל־בֵּית֖וֹ לֹ֥א יָרָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David called him to eat and to drink before him, and he made him drunk: and he went out in the evening, and slept on his couch with the servants of his lord, and went not down into his house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David kutsui hänen kanssansa syömään ja juomaan, ja juovutti hänen. Ja ehtoona läksi hän ulos ja pani vuoteesensa levätä herransa palveliain kanssa, ja ei mennyt huoneesensa.
verses.king_james: And when David had called him, he did eat and drink before him; and he made him drunk: and at even he went out to lie on his bed with the servants of his lord, but went not down to his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est ergo mane, et scripsit David epistolam ad Joab : misitque per manum Uriæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י בַבֹּ֔קֶר וַיִּכְתֹּ֥ב דָּוִ֛ד סֵ֖פֶר אֶל־יוֹאָ֑ב וַיִּשְׁלַ֖ח בְּיַ֥ד אוּרִיָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the morning was come, David wrote a letter to Joab: and sent it by the hand of Urias,
verses.finnish_biblia: Huomeneltain kirjoitti David Joabin tykö kirjan, ja lähetti sen Urian myötä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in the morning, that David wrote a letter to Joab, and sent it by the hand of Uriah.

verses.latin_vulgate: scribens in epistola : Ponite Uriam ex adverso belli, ubi fortissimum est prælium : et derelinquite eum, ut percussus intereat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּכְתֹּ֥ב בַּסֵּ֖פֶר לֵאמֹ֑ר הָב֣וּ אֶת־אוּרִיָּ֗ה אֶל־מוּל֙ פְּנֵ֤י הַמִּלְחָמָה֙ הַחֲזָקָ֔ה וְשַׁבְתֶּ֥ם מֵאַחֲרָ֖יו וְנִכָּ֥ה וָמֵֽת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Writing in the letter: Set ye Urias in the front of the battle, where the fight is strongest: and leave ye him, that he may be wounded and die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän kirjoitti kirjaan sanoen: pankaat Uria kovimman sodan eteen, ja kääntykäät hänen tyköänsä takaperin, että hän lyötäisiin ja kuolis.
verses.king_james: And he wrote in the letter, saying, Set ye Uriah in the forefront of the hottest battle, and retire ye from him, that he may be smitten, and die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur cum Joab obsideret urbem, posuit Uriam in loco ubi sciebat viros esse fortissimos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י בִּשְׁמ֥וֹר יוֹאָ֖ב אֶל־הָעִ֑יר וַיִּתֵּן֙ אֶת־א֣וּרִיָּ֔ה אֶל־הַמָּקוֹם֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר יָדַ֔ע כִּ֥י אַנְשֵׁי־חַ֖יִל שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore as Joab was besieging the city, he put Urias in the place where he knew the bravest men were.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Joab oli kaupungin edessä, asetti hän Urian siihen paikkaan, kussa hän väkevimmät sotamiehet tiesi olevan.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Joab observed the city, that he assigned Uriah unto a place where he knew that valiant men were.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressique viri de civitate, bellabant adversum Joab, et ceciderunt de populo servorum David, et mortuus est etiam Urias Hethæus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֨צְא֜וּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י הָעִיר֙ וַיִּלָּחֲמ֣וּ אֶת־יוֹאָ֔ב וַיִּפֹּ֥ל מִן־הָעָ֖ם מֵעַבְדֵ֣י דָוִ֑ד וַיָּ֕מׇת גַּ֖ם אוּרִיָּ֥ה הַחִתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the men coming out of the city, fought against Joab, and there fell some of the people of the servants of David, and Urias the Hethite was killed also.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin miehet läksivät kaupungista ja sotivat Joabia vastaan, kaatuivat muutamat Davidin palvelioista, ja Uria Hetiläinen myös kuoli.
verses.king_james: And the men of the city went out, and fought with Joab: and there fell some of the people of the servants of David; and Uriah the Hittite died also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit itaque Joab, et nuntiavit David omnia verba prælii :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֖ח יוֹאָ֑ב וַיַּגֵּ֣ד לְדָוִ֔ד אֶת־כׇּל־דִּבְרֵ֖י הַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Joab sent, and told David all things concerning the battle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joab lähetti ja antoi sanoa Davidille kaikki sodan menot,
verses.king_james: Then Joab sent and told David all the things concerning the war;

verses.latin_vulgate: præcepitque nuntio, dicens : Cum compleveris universos sermones belli ad regem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְצַ֥ו אֶת־הַמַּלְאָ֖ךְ לֵאמֹ֑ר כְּכַלּוֹתְךָ֗ אֵ֛ת כׇּל־דִּבְרֵ֥י הַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה לְדַבֵּ֥ר אֶל־הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he charged the messenger, saying: When thou hast told all the words of the battle to the king,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja käski sanansaattajalle sanoen: kuin sinä olet puhunut kaikki sodan menot kuninkaalle:
verses.king_james: And charged the messenger, saying, When thou hast made an end of telling the matters of the war unto the king,

verses.latin_vulgate: si eum videris indignari, et dixerit : Quare accessistis ad murum, ut præliaremini ? an ignorabatis quod multa desuper ex muro tela mittantur ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֗ה אִֽם־תַּעֲלֶה֙ חֲמַ֣ת הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וְאָמַ֣ר לְךָ֔ מַדּ֛וּעַ נִגַּשְׁתֶּ֥ם אֶל־הָעִ֖יר לְהִלָּחֵ֑ם הֲל֣וֹא יְדַעְתֶּ֔ם אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־יֹר֖וּ מֵעַ֥ל הַחוֹמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: If thou see him to be angry, and he shall say: Why did you approach so near to the wall to fight? knew you not that many darts are thrown from above off the wall?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja jos niin on, että kuningas vihastuu ja sanoo sinulle: miksi te niin lähes kaupunkia astuitte sodalla? Ettekö te tietäneet, että muurin päältä paiskataan?
verses.king_james: And if so be that the king’s wrath arise, and he say unto thee, Wherefore approached ye so nigh unto the city when ye did fight? knew ye not that they would shoot from the wall?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis percussit Abimelech filium Jerobaal ? nonne mulier misit super eum fragmen molæ de muro, et interfecit eum in Thebes ? quare juxta murum accessistis ? dices : Etiam servus tuus Urias Hethæus occubuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־הִכָּ֞ה אֶת־אֲבִימֶ֣לֶךְ בֶּן־יְרֻבֶּ֗שֶׁת הֲלֽוֹא־אִשָּׁ֡ה הִשְׁלִ֣יכָה עָלָיו֩ פֶּ֨לַח רֶ֜כֶב מֵעַ֤ל הַחוֹמָה֙ וַיָּ֣מׇת בְּתֵבֵ֔ץ לָ֥מָּה נִגַּשְׁתֶּ֖ם אֶל־הַחוֹמָ֑ה וְאָ֣מַרְתָּ֔ גַּ֗ם עַבְדְּךָ֛ אוּרִיָּ֥ה הַחִתִּ֖י מֵֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who killed Abimelech the son of Jerobaal? did not a woman cast a piece of a millstone upon him from the wall and slew him in Thebes? Why did you go near the wall? Thou shalt say: Thy servant Urias the Hethite is also slain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka löi Abimelekin Jerubbesetin pojan? Eikö vaimo paiskannut myllynkiven kappaletta muurista hänen päällensä, että hän kuoli Tebetsessä? Miksi te astuitte niin lähes muuria? Niin sano: sinun palvelias Uria Hetiläinen on myös kuollut.
verses.king_james: Who smote Abimelech the son of Jerubbesheth? did not a woman cast a piece of a millstone upon him from the wall, that he died in Thebez? why went ye nigh the wall? then say thou, Thy servant Uriah the Hittite is dead also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit ergo nuntius, et venit, et narravit David omnia quæ ei præceperat Joab.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֖לֶךְ הַמַּלְאָ֑ךְ וַיָּבֹא֙ וַיַּגֵּ֣ד לְדָוִ֔ד אֵ֛ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר שְׁלָח֖וֹ יוֹאָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the messenger departed, and came and told David all that Joab had commanded him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sanansaattaja meni ja tuli ja jutteli Davidille kaikki nämät, joiden tähden Joab hänen lähettänyt oli.
verses.king_james: So the messenger went, and came and shewed David all that Joab had sent him for.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit nuntius ad David : Prævaluerunt adversum nos viri, et egressi sunt ad nos in agrum : nos autem facto impetu persecuti eos sumus usque ad portam civitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר הַמַּלְאָךְ֙ אֶל־דָּוִ֔ד כִּֽי־גָבְר֤וּ עָלֵ֙ינוּ֙ הָאֲנָשִׁ֔ים וַיֵּצְא֥וּ אֵלֵ֖ינוּ הַשָּׂדֶ֑ה וַנִּהְיֶ֥ה עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם עַד־פֶּ֥תַח הַשָּֽׁעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the messenger said to David: The men prevailed against us, and they came out to us into the field: and we vigorously charged and pursued them even to the gate of the city.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanansaattaja sanoi Davidille: ne miehet voittivat meidät ja tulivat ulos kedolle meidän tykömme; mutta me löimme heidät takaperin kaupungin porttiin asti.
verses.king_james: And the messenger said unto David, Surely the men prevailed against us, and came out unto us into the field, and we were upon them even unto the entering of the gate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et direxerunt jacula sagittarii ad servos tuos ex muro desuper, mortuique sunt de servis regis : quin etiam servus tuus Urias Hethæus mortuus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּר֨אוּ הַמּוֹרִ֤אים אֶל־עֲבָדֶ֙יךָ֙ מֵעַ֣ל הַחוֹמָ֔ה וַיָּמ֖וּתוּ מֵעַבְדֵ֣י הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וְגַ֗ם עַבְדְּךָ֛ אוּרִיָּ֥ה הַחִתִּ֖י מֵֽת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the archers shot their arrows at thy servants from off the wall above: and some of the king's servants are slain, and thy servant Urias the Hethite is also dead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ampujat ampuivat muurista sinun palvelloitas ja tappoivat muutamia kuninkaan palvelioita, ja sinun palvelias Uria Hetiläinen on myös kuollut.
verses.king_james: And the shooters shot from off the wall upon thy servants; and some of the king’s servants be dead, and thy servant Uriah the Hittite is dead also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit David ad nuntium : Hæc dices Joab : Non te frangat ista res : varius enim eventus est belli, nunc hunc, et nunc illum consumit gladius : conforta bellatores tuos adversus urbem ut destruas eam, et exhortare eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜ד אֶל־הַמַּלְאָ֗ךְ כֹּֽה־תֹאמַ֤ר אֶל־יוֹאָב֙ אַל־יֵרַ֤ע בְּעֵינֶ֙יךָ֙ אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה כִּֽי־כָזֹ֥ה וְכָזֶ֖ה תֹּאכַ֣ל הֶחָ֑רֶב הַחֲזֵ֨ק מִלְחַמְתְּךָ֧ אֶל־הָעִ֛יר וְהׇרְסָ֖הּ וְחַזְּקֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to the messenger: Thus shalt thou say to Joab: Let not this thing discourage thee: for various is the event of war: and sometimes one, sometimes another is consumed by the sword: encourage thy warriors against the city, and exhort them that thou mayest overthrow it.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi sanansaattajalle: sano näin Joabille: älä sitä pahakses pane, sillä miekka syö nyt yhden, nyt toisen, vahvista sota kaupunkia vastaan, ettäs kukistaisit sen: ja vahvista sinä häntä.
verses.king_james: Then David said unto the messenger, Thus shalt thou say unto Joab, Let not this thing displease thee, for the sword devoureth one as well as another: make thy battle more strong against the city, and overthrow it: and encourage thou him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit autem uxor Uriæ quod mortuus esset Urias vir suus, et planxit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּשְׁמַע֙ אֵ֣שֶׁת אוּרִיָּ֔ה כִּי־מֵ֖ת אוּרִיָּ֣ה אִישָׁ֑הּ וַתִּסְפֹּ֖ד עַל־בַּעְלָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the wife of Urias heard that Urias her husband was dead, and she mourned for him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Urian emäntä kuuli miehensä Urian kuolleeksi, murehti hän miestänsä.
verses.king_james: And when the wife of Uriah heard that Uriah her husband was dead, she mourned for her husband.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transacto autem luctu, misit David, et introduxit eam in domum suam, et facta est ei uxor, peperitque ei filium : et displicuit verbum hoc quod fecerat David, coram Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲבֹ֣ר הָאֵ֗בֶל וַיִּשְׁלַ֨ח דָּוִ֜ד וַיַּאַסְפָ֤הּ אֶל־בֵּיתוֹ֙ וַתְּהִי־ל֣וֹ לְאִשָּׁ֔ה וַתֵּ֥לֶד ל֖וֹ בֵּ֑ן וַיֵּ֧רַע הַדָּבָ֛ר אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֥ה דָוִ֖ד בְּעֵינֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the mourning being over, David sent and brought her into his house, and she became his wife, and she bore him a son: and this thing which David had done, was displeasing to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sittekuin hän oli murehtinut, lähetti David ja antoi tuoda hänen huoneesensa, ja hän tuli hänen emännäksensä ja synnytti hänelle pojan, mutta se työ, jonka David teki, oli paha Herran edessä.
verses.king_james: And when the mourning was past, David sent and fetched her to his house, and she became his wife, and bare him a son. But the thing that David had done displeased the Lord.
